language_prompt: Choose the bot language for this chat.
start_intro:
Hi! Add me to your trip chat, enable auto-drafts in /settings, and discuss the trip in normal messages.

I can quickly build a draft: where, when, how many people, weather, and where to look for tickets and stays.

Main commands:
• /summary — current trip plan
• /tickets — ticket prices via Travelpayouts
• /status — your reply for the trip
• /plan — start a manual brief and send the description in the next message
• /settings — enable or disable auto-drafts
• /hotels — quick housing flow
• /trips — trip history
• /help — short guide
help_text: How to use 1. Add the bot to a group chat. 2. Enable auto-drafts in /settings. 3. Discuss the trip in normal messages. 4. Open /summary when you need the current plan. Main commands • /summary — active trip summary • /tickets — ticket prices and budget fit • /status — your reply for the trip • /plan — start a manual brief and send the trip description in the next message • /settings — chat mode • /hotels — housing and search prep • /trips — trip history • /select_trip ID — restore a trip from history What the bot does now • builds a trip from chat discussion • shows weather • pulls ticket prices via Travelpayouts when the departure city is known • suggests links by route: local services for RU/CIS and international ones abroad • stores the active trip and history Limitations • the bot does not book tickets or housing • prices are checked on external sites
settings_explainer: Auto-drafts let the bot suggest a plan when it sees a travel discussion in the group. How it works • the bot only analyzes messages it has seen after being added to the group and after auto-drafts were enabled • Telegram does not give the bot old chat history retroactively • the bot uses a recent message window and keeps updating the active trip as new messages arrive If you want a command-only mode, turn this off.
status_prompt_intro: Your reply for the active trip in this chat:
status_interested_hint: 🤔 Thinking — I have not decided yet
status_not_going_hint: ❌ Not going — I am not joining
status_footer: This affects the group composition and helps the bot build a more accurate plan. Route, edit, and delete are available in the trip card.
participants_none: ⏳ No one has marked a status yet.
participants_none_progress: ⏳ No one has marked a status yet. Progress: 0/{known}.
detected_needs_intro: 🧩 From the chat I can see
summary_route_button_note: Opens via the separate button below.
summary_use_summary: Open /summary if you need the full plan.
summary_short_no_destination: The destination is not clear yet. Once the city and dates appear, the bot will rebuild the route and links.
group_wait_destination: I'm waiting for the destination before building a meaningful route, housing suggestions, and useful links.
group_open_questions: What still needs to be clarified
group_clarify_destination: Looks like you're discussing a trip. Where do you want to go?
group_vote_intro: The chat is still split on the destination, so I'm not building the route yet. These are the options I see now {rendered} Once one option clearly leads, I'll build one active trip for the group.
group_vote_fallback: I can see the destination is not decided yet. Tell me which city is the main option now.
trip_created_new_group: Built a new plan for this group.
trip_created_replaced_group: Built a new plan and made it active for this group. The previous one is saved in history.
trip_created_replaced_private: Built a new plan and made it active. The previous one is saved in history.
route_empty: The route has not been built yet.
tickets_empty: No ticket data yet. Specify the departure city and dates.
housing_empty: No housing data yet. Specify the destination and dates.
trip_list_empty: There are no trips in this chat yet.
trip_list_hint: You can open or delete a trip using the buttons below.
active_trip_missing: There is no active trip yet. Run /plan and send the description in the next message, or create it step by step with /newtrip.
plan_prompt:
Send a free-form trip description in the next message.

It helps to include:
• where you want to go
• how many people are going
• departure city / starting point
• outbound and return dates or an approximate date range
• budget
• interests
• if you only need a one-way ticket, say: 'one way' or 'no return ticket'
• if you already know the stay format, you can include it too: hotel, apartment, flat, house

If some details are missing, the bot may ask 1-2 short follow-up questions. For example: about the departure city, ticket format, or housing.

Example:
I want to go to Vladivostok with friends for 5 days. There are 4 of us, flying from Novosibirsk, outbound on June 12, return on June 16, economy budget, and we love the sea and food.

One-way example:
I want to go to Istanbul alone, flying from Tbilisi on June 12, one-way ticket, business budget, interested in walks and food.
housing_try_later: I have no options yet. Try again later or open /summary.
